title: Two-Rook Ladder
setup: Both rooks are on the **b-file**, ready to climb the ladder side by side.
hint: One rook delivers check on the 8th rank while the other guards {{b7}} — no escape between them.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is the **two-rook ladder mate** — also called the *staircase*. With two rooks, you alternate ranks so the king is squeezed against the edge with no air.

Play {{Rb8#}}. The b-rook climbs to **b8** and gives check along the 8th rank.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{g8}}, {{f8}} and the rest of the 8th rank — all swept by the new rook on b8
- {{g7}} — blocked by Black's own pawn
- {{h7}} — blocked by Black's own pawn
- The lower rook on **b2** still rules the b-file, so Kxb8 would be illegal anyway (not that the king could reach)

> **Takeaway:** Two rooks are a mating machine even without a king. Keep them on adjacent files or ranks and march them up — the loose king has nowhere to run.
